meaning: n.(剧场等)衣帽间,(车站等)行李站暂存处,<英>盥洗室,便所
examples: I went to the cloakroom to pick up our coats.
Each apartment was designed with an antechamber, a chamber, a cabinet and a cloakroom.
He deposited his overcoat in the cloakroom.
George wasn't in them then - he used to be on the cloakroom with me.
To buy his own drum set, Desmond worked in the cloakroom of Manor House, a popular London music venue in the 1960s.
